title: Handed
content:
Handed

• Where the protected party resides: some states allow victims to file orders in their home state, even if the abuser lives elsewhere.

NY/NJ Residence

• Type of restraining order needed: Emergency? Temporary? Domestic violence? Civil harassment?

Florida-Specific Notes:
• Florida permits restraining orders (called “injunctions for protection”) for out-of-state abuse if the petitioner resides in Florida and can show ongoing risk or jurisdictional connection.
• He may need to file in both states if there’s cross-jurisdictional risk or action.

Recommendation: Contact both:
• A Florida family law attorney (for protective injunctions)
context: From document: Restraining Order / Legal Referral — chunk 2 (chunk 2/4)
